description: At the market, eggs go one way and honey returns the other. 'wisola' is an exchange in which participants each give and receive something within the understood arrangement. It does not promise equal amounts or a fair bargain.
articulatory_notes: Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', then move forward and unround into high-front 'i'. Bring the tongue near the alveolar ridge and pass unvoiced air through a narrow central channel for 's', then round the lips for stressed middle-height back 'o'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for voiced 'l', let air pass along its sides, and finish with open central 'a'.
sound_symbolism: Reciprocal 'wiso' (each other) occupies the first two syllables intact. Final 'la' carries that compact form into one more open syllable, an easy mnemonic for exchange moving between participants.
usage_notes: A plural subject can exchange, and reciprocal 'wiso' makes action towards one another explicit. A partner can take comitative 'nua', while the exchanged material or activity is the direct object. The event-noun rule supplies an exchange. 'loa' (give) may name one direction within it, and 'phowe' (share) concerns use with others rather than paired transfer.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Seed swaps and tool exchanges can circulate useful things without a price. 'wisola' still leaves the participants free to judge whether the arrangement met their needs.

semantic_domains.activity: participants giving and receiving within one reciprocal arrangement
semantic_domains.communication: exchanging stories, knowledge, or other communicative material
semantic_domains.community: reciprocal transfer among people or groups
